chief_complaint: An 81-year-old male presented with persistent substernal crushing pain.
history: The patient had a history of refractory hypertension, low BMI, and a smoking history. He presented with elevated cardiac biomarkers, leading to an initial diagnosis of acute coronary syndrome (ACS).
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: ECG: not specified; Bloods: elevated cardiac biomarkers, d-dimer level within normal limits; Imaging: coronary angiography revealed no obstructive disease but a hazy appearance in the left anterior descending artery; IVUS interrogation of the left main ostium identified a fibrous plaque with a widened hypoechoic space extending toward the aorta; Aortic computed tomography angiography (CTA) visualized the dissection flap.
differentials: Aortic Dissection Involving the Coronary Arteries
correct_ranking: Aortic Dissection Involving the Coronary Arteries
